digest.component_or_area: rebar terraform infra (shared AWS resource adoption)
propositions: 18 shared AWS resources owned by the decommissioned snap project must be re-homed into rebar's own terraform and state.
The GitHub OIDC provider is a per-account singleton that rebar's drift role depends on but does not create; destroying snap's dead state would break rebar drift CI.
The wildcard ACM cert and domain-wide auth_host SSO stack are idle orphans in snap's dead state and should survive by being adopted into rebar.
Physical resource names (snap-demo-*) must be preserved to avoid destroy/recreate; only ownership and the project tag change to Project=rebar.
Adopted resources must be imported into rebar state, retagged, and released from snap's shared state, with a clean terraform plan showing no destroy/replace or secret rotation.
rebar infra docs must document OIDC ownership and the SSO runbook, with child stories closed and changes landed on main via Gerrit.
